title: OSMM Messaging Framework Object
description: The message architecture for an offering or campaign: the primary message, the supporting message pillars, the evidence that substantiates them, and the persona-differentiated message variants. Canonical schema for object_type 'messaging_framework'. The osmm-messaging-framework-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape. Messaging is differentiated by Persona (variants key to a linked Persona) and sources its raw material from Product Context's product_messaging; it carries no audience-membership logic (delivery is owned by Campaign Strategy).

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

messaging_framework_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'MSF-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: A readable label for the framework, usually entity + offering (e.g. 'IBM watsonx Messaging Framework').

primary_message.description: The single overarching message — the apex of the hierarchy. The one thing every variant ladders up to (5.1).

message_pillars.description: The supporting pillars beneath the primary message — the message hierarchy's middle tier (5.1/5.4).

pillar.description: The pillar's name — a single supporting theme that holds up the primary message.

support.description: How this pillar substantiates the primary message — the argument it makes.

proof_points.description: Pillar-level evidence (stats, certifications, named customers, awards). Optional.
Framework-level evidence substantiating the messaging overall. Optional.

value_framing.description: How value is framed overall — the lens through which the offering's worth is communicated. Optional.

persona_variants.description: The persona-differentiated message variants — the core of this object. One entry per Persona this framework speaks to; the message is differentiated BY persona (not by audience).

linked_persona.description: Id of the Persona this variant is tuned to ('PER-<slug>'; 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until that object exists). The differentiation key.

angle.description: The framing angle for this persona — which of their motivations/pain points/decision criteria the message leans into.

key_message.description: The single message that lands hardest for this persona — the primary message, re-expressed for them.

tone.description: How to sound for this persona, within the brand voice (which lives in Brand Context). Optional.

emphasis.description: Which pillars/proof points to foreground for this persona. Optional.

tone_principles.description: Framework-level tone guidance. The durable brand voice lives in Brand Context; reference it rather than redefining it here. Optional.
tone_principles.$comment: Brand voice/guardrails are owned by Brand Context (linked_brand_context); these are framework-scoped notes only.

linked_product.description: Id of the Product Context whose 'product_messaging' this framework draws from. 'PRD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_personas.description: Ids of the Persona objects this framework serves. Each persona_variants entry keys to one of these. 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_brand_context.description: Id of the Brand Context that owns voice and guardrails. 'BRC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Id of the Campaign Strategy if this framework is scoped to a specific campaign. 'CMS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
